creator: karl@denninger.net
text:
If I remove the openssl port (e.g. pkg delete openssl) the build completes.

If I install the openssl port from security/openssl first both samba43 and samba44 fail with the above.  Whether I have them declared in /etc/make.conf does not change the outcome.

I have gone as far as doing a "make clean" in /usr/ports (which took a while) to make sure that there was no old cruft that might be involved.  On both a production and my test machine this is consistent and repeatable.

Whether I remove samba first before attempting to build it doesn't change the outcome either (pkg delete samba43 did *not* allow the build to complete, and on the production machine it of course did exactly what it was told to); fortunately I am ok with the default options so a "pkg add" was able to restore it without having to roll back the snapshot on the /usr/local/ filesystem.

root@NewFS:/usr/ports/net/samba44 # openssl version
OpenSSL 1.0.2h-freebsd  3 May 2016

Same version of openssl as Gerald is running.
